library_preparation_preflight.summary: Jersey 1.9 Grizzly2 can be exercised with a local Grizzly HTTP server, but documented startup on JDK 21 reaches Jersey WADL/JAXB initialization and fails without JAXB classes, which are no longer in the JDK.

deterministic_setup.reason: A minimal GrizzlyServerFactory server using com.sun.jersey:jersey-grizzly2:1.9 fails on JDK 21 with NoClassDefFoundError: javax/xml/bind/JAXBException during Jersey WADL initialization; this API dependency restores the missing javax.xml.bind classes needed for normal server startup.

library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: Use an in-test JAX-RS resource, start org.glassfish.grizzly.http.server.HttpServer on 127.0.0.1 with an ephemeral port, send requests with JDK HttpURLConnection or similar, and stop the server in cleanup. Avoid asserting /application.wadl unless a JAXB implementation/runtime is also deliberately added; simple resource request coverage does not need Docker or external services.
risks: Jersey 1.9 and Grizzly 2.1.2 are old; native-image may still require generated reachability metadata for Jersey resource reflection and Grizzly internals.
The JAXB API dependency is enough for ordinary resource endpoint startup and requests, but WADL response generation needs a JAXB implementation such as jaxb-runtime.
